commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1e52968e269df289ba89155c17d1af6ea5fe23bf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR caps `acquireSessionWriteLock`'s effective `maxHoldMs` by the acquire timeout and adds lock-payload regression tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +56. Total +74 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect by source inspection: current code rejects zero min/grace overrides, so the new test expecting a 60000 ms payload should fail and the reported default behavior remains 300000 ms. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the patch has blocking code findings, missing real behavior proof, and a compatibility-sensitive maxHoldMs policy choice.

target.title: fix(session-write-lock): bound maxHoldMs by acquire timeout
target.body:
## Summary

The session write lock's `maxHoldMs` defaulted to 300 s (5 min) regardless of the acquire `timeoutMs` (60 s). This created a situation where a plugin hook (e.g. `active-memory` sub-agent in `before_prompt_build`) could hold the lock for the full 5 min while other operations timed out after 60 s, producing `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` for every concurrent operation that shares the same lock file (e.g. `mirrorCodexAppServerTranscript`).

The fix bounds `maxHoldMs` in `acquireSessionWriteLock` by `timeoutMs` (using `resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout` with `minMs: 0, graceMs: 0`), so a holder cannot block other acquirers past their configured acquire timeout. Explicit `maxHoldMs` values below the bound are still honored.

## Changes

- `src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`: in `acquireSessionWriteLock`, compute `maxHoldFromTimeout = resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout({ timeoutMs, minMs: 0, graceMs: 0 })` and use `Math.min(resolvedMaxHoldMs, maxHoldFromTimeout)` as the effective hold limit. With default 60 s timeout, `maxHoldMs` is now 60 s instead of 300 s.
- `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`: added two tests proving the bounding behavior (60 s timeout → 60 s maxHoldMs) and that explicit low `maxHoldMs` values are preserved.

## Reproduction

Before fix — lock file shows `maxHoldMs: 300000` even with 60 s timeout:
```json
{"pid": 1302, "createdAt": "2026-06-24T16:31:53.605Z", "maxHoldMs": 300000}
```

After fix — lock file shows `maxHoldMs: 60000` matching the 60 s acquire timeout:
```json
{"pid": 1302, "createdAt": "2026-06-24T16:31:53.605Z", "maxHoldMs": 60000}
```

## Proof

Test `acquireSessionWriteLock bounds maxHoldMs by timeoutMs` in `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts` verifies the lock payload records `maxHoldMs: 60000` when `timeoutMs: 60_000` is passed (without explicit `maxHoldMs` override).
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
